text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check small pieces of code, like individual functions, while integration tests ensure different parts of the system work together correctly. Using test-driven development (TDD), developers write a test before writing the actual code, which helps them think clearly about the desired behavior. This approach leads to more reliable code and reduces the time spent debugging later. Most importantly, a good testing strategy gives the entire team confidence. When changes are made, the tests quickly catch any problems, allowing developers to refactor and improve the software without fear of breaking existing features.
